title: fabric-monorepo own fabric-config — set knowledge_language=zh-CN
description: Set fabric-monorepo's own `.fabric/fabric-config.json` to declare `knowledge_language: 'zh-CN'` — the dogfood declaration that fabric-monorepo eats its own zh-CN baseline. If file does not yet exist, create it with minimal valid shape; if it exists, add the new field while preserving existing keys. After this commit: fabric init on this repo (re-running) would produce zh-CN baselines; doctor must report clean.

files.change: If file exists: add `"knowledge_language": "zh-CN"` to the JSON object, preserving all existing keys + formatting (2-space indent). If file does not exist: create with minimal valid shape — `{"knowledge_language": "zh-CN"}` plus any other required fields per fabric-config schema (likely just clients list — check schema for required fields).

implementation: If exists: read current content, parse as JSON, add `knowledge_language: 'zh-CN'` field at appropriate position, write back with consistent formatting (2-space indent matching existing convention)

If absent: examine fabric-config Zod schema (TASK-002 output) to identify required fields; create minimal config containing knowledge_language=zh-CN + any required fields (likely clients: ['claudeCode'] or similar default)

Run schema validation manually: `node -e "const {FabricConfigSchema} = require('./packages/shared/dist/schemas/fabric-config'); const cfg = require('./.fabric/fabric-config.json'); console.log(FabricConfigSchema.parse(cfg))"` — confirms file parses cleanly under TASK-002's extended schema
Run `node packages/cli/dist/index.js doctor` — confirm exit 0 (no validation errors)
Run `git diff .fabric/fabric-config.json` — eyeball the change is minimal and surgical
Run `pnpm test` — confirm no test regressions

reference.pattern: Project-config dogfood declaration — single field add to JSON config
reference.examples: If creating, follow minimal-config conventions in fabric-config schema; if editing, preserve existing key ordering

criteria: `git diff` shows minimal/surgical change (only 1 field added or 1 file created)

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: re-running init to regenerate baselines from new config (would clobber TASK-007 manual rewrites — explicitly avoided); modifying schema (TASK-002); modifying init template (TASK-008); modifying any other config file. Strictly: 1 field add (or 1 file create) in fabric-monorepo's own fabric-config.json.

rationale.chosen_approach: Single-field config addition declaring zh-CN language preference for fabric-monorepo dogfood — cheap, atomic, validates the new schema field end-to-end
alternatives_considered: Skip (loses dogfood declaration — fabric-monorepo would not demonstrate the new field in production)
Full config rewrite (out of scope, no need)
Re-run init to regenerate baselines (would conflict with TASK-007 manual rewrites)
decision_factors: Dogfood credibility — fabric-monorepo's own config declares its own language preference
Validates TASK-002 + TASK-008 wiring end-to-end (config flows through schema and through init)
rationale.tradeoffs: If fabric-config.json doesn't currently exist in this repo, we create it — slightly bigger change than 'edit existing'. Per `ls .fabric/` output, file is currently absent (only agents.meta.json/audit.jsonl/events.jsonl/forensic.json/knowledge/ present), so creation is required.

manual_checks: Eyeball `.fabric/fabric-config.json` — confirm 1 field add or minimal-shape new file
Run schema validation manually — confirm parses under extended schema
success_metrics: Surgical diff (only field add or new file)
integration: fabric-config flows through TASK-002 schema parsing
doctor accepts the config without errors
